mechanism_narrative: Rising PAYEMS indicates expanding payrolls and wage income, which drives consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between PAYEMS changes and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A UNRATE reading above 7.0 percent signals a deteriorating labor market that weakens household income expectations and compresses corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of observed months when UNRATE exceeds 7.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated CPIAUCSL and suppressed FEDFUNDS create deeply negative real interest rates that redirect capital from fixed income into equities and inflate nominal earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with elevated inflation
failure_condition: The interaction of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS adds less than 1 percentage point of incremental R-squared versus single-factor models over any 3-year window, or 60-day returns are negative in more than 40 percent of triggered quarters.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime, increases in HOUST signal early-cycle stimulus transmission into construction and durable goods orders, which lifts broad earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Increases in HOUST during periods when UNRATE exceeds 6.5 percent are followed by non-positive mean 60-day SPX returns with a t-test p-value above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in INDPRO indicates contracting manufacturing output and falling capacity utilization, which reduces industrial corporate profits and drags the S&P 500 lower over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in INDPRO coincide with positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 45 percent of observations, indicating no negative drift.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in RSAFS reflects accelerating consumer spending that immediately boosts near-term revenue expectations for retail and consumer discretionary sectors, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in RSAFS coincide with negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 45 percent of observations, indicating no positive drift.

mechanism_narrative: A GDPC1 level above 15500 billion chained 2017 dollars signals that aggregate demand has recovered sufficiently to sustain broad corporate profitability, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 above 15500 is followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 45 percent of quarterly observations, or the mean return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of UNRATE above 7.0 percent and FEDFUNDS near zero indicates a liquidity trap with severe labor market deterioration, triggering flight from risk assets and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: The joint condition of UNRATE above 7.0 percent and FEDFUNDS below 0.2 percent fails to produce negative 20-day returns more frequently than UNRATE alone by at least 8 percentage points.
